seoTitle: How to Create a Ticket in Zendesk Step by Step
metaDescription: Quickly log a support request in Zendesk by opening a new ticket, setting the requester and owner, and submitting it with the right status and details.
intro: Zendesk tickets don't always start from an inbound email — sometimes you need to log a request yourself after a call or a hallway conversation. This guide covers opening a fresh ticket tab, filling in the requester and owner fields, and submitting it so it lands in the right queue.

steps.heading: Start a fresh ticket
steps.body: Click the "+ Add" tab in your top navigation bar to open a blank ticket.

steps.heading: Identify the requester
steps.body: Search existing contacts or register a new customer profile using their name or email address.

steps.heading: Route the ticket
steps.body: Set the Group and Assignee fields so the ticket lands with the right team or individual agent.

steps.heading: Describe the issue
steps.body: Write a clear subject line and capture the core issue details in the ticket body.

steps.heading: Categorize the ticket
steps.body: Adjust the Type, Priority, and Brand dropdowns to keep your reporting accurate.

steps.heading: Submit the ticket
steps.body: Pick a status like New, Open, or Pending and submit to finalize the record.

faq.question: Where do I find the option to start a new ticket?
faq.answer: The "+ Add" tab control sits in the top navigation bar of the Zendesk Support workspace.

faq.question: What if I don't know the customer's exact email?
faq.answer: You can search by partial name or email in the requester field, or register a new profile if they don't already exist.

faq.question: Do I have to set a priority level right away?
faq.answer: It's not strictly required, but setting Type and Priority helps keep your queue organized and reporting accurate.

faq.question: Can I create a ticket on behalf of a coworker's request?
faq.answer: Yes, you can set the requester field to any registered contact regardless of who is submitting the ticket on their behalf.
